Class SudokuSolver

java.lang.Object
sudoku.SudokuSolver
All Implemented Interfaces:
ISudokuSolver
public class SudokuSolver extends Object implements ISudokuSolver
Solves a Sudoku grid using recursive backtracking.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SudokuSolver(ISudokuGrid grid)
    Creates a solver which operates on the given grid.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    countSolutions()
    Calculates the number of solutions that a sudoku puzzle currently has.
    boolean
    hasUniqueSolution()
    Determines whether or not the sudoku puzzle has exactly one valid solution.
    boolean
    isSolvable()
    Uses backtracking to compute whether or not the grid in its current state is solvable.
    boolean
    isValidMove(int row, int column, int value)
    Checks whether setting a given cell to a certain value would follow the rules of Sudoku.
    Set<ISudokuGrid>
    solveGrid()
    Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.
    Set<ISudokuGrid>
    solveGrid(int solutions_required)
    Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • SudokuSolver

      public SudokuSolver(ISudokuGrid grid)
      Creates a solver which operates on the given grid.
      Parameters:
      grid - The grid to solve. The grid itself is not modified; solving methods operate on clones of it.

  • Method Details

    • isSolvable

      public boolean isSolvable()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuSolver
      Uses backtracking to compute whether or not the grid in its current state is solvable.
      Specified by:
      isSolvable in interface ISudokuSolver
      Returns:
      whether or not the sudoku puzzle is solvable

    • hasUniqueSolution

      public boolean hasUniqueSolution()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuSolver
      Determines whether or not the sudoku puzzle has exactly one valid solution.
      Specified by:
      hasUniqueSolution in interface ISudokuSolver
      Returns:
      true if the puzzle has one solution; false otherwise

    • countSolutions

      public int countSolutions()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuSolver
      Calculates the number of solutions that a sudoku puzzle currently has.

      Note that this method is only safe to call on grids which are near-complete, as calling on a grid with many possible solutions may result in an OutOfMemoryError.

      Specified by:
      countSolutions in interface ISudokuSolver
      Returns:
      the number of possible solutions of the grid in its current state.

    • solveGrid

      public Set<ISudokuGrid> solveGrid()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuSolver
      Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.
      Specified by:
      solveGrid in interface ISudokuSolver
      Returns:
      the (possibly empty) set of all possible solutions for the grid.

    • solveGrid

      public Set<ISudokuGrid> solveGrid(int solutions_required)
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuSolver
      Computes a set of solutions for the sudoku grid in its current state.
      Specified by:
      solveGrid in interface ISudokuSolver
      Parameters:
      solutions_required - The maximum number of solutions that should be generated.
      Returns:
      the (possibly empty) set of possible solutions for the grid.

    • isValidMove

      public boolean isValidMove(int row, int column, int value)
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuSolver
      Checks whether setting a given cell to a certain value would follow the rules of Sudoku.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Specified by:
      isValidMove in interface ISudokuSolver
      Parameters:
      row - the row (y-coordinate) of the cell to check.
      column - the column (x-coordinate) of the cell to check.
      value - the value to test.
      Returns:
      whether the value can be legally placed in the cell
